events.label: assistant
events.text:
Wrote the assessment to `/app/answer.json`. 

Summary: the floating floor was installed without the required perimeter expansion gap (typically 1/4"–3/8" per manufacturer/TCNA guidance), which is why it's buckling — the panels have no room to expand with temperature/humidity changes. This is a **fail** at high risk since it will progressively worsen and create a trip hazard if trim is closed out over it. Corrective action is to pull back the base/trim, cut proper expansion clearance at all fixed obstructions, and confirm the floor relaxes before final trim-out.
